title: Diapers - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Parenting & Childcare > Diapers 65 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Foster Children 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details AFRICAN FAMILY HOLISTIC HEALTH ORGANIZATION WATOTO WETU MATERNAL PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides childbirth and prenatal classes, doula referrals, and baby showers with gifts to participants. Supports parents with emotional, social and economic support. Offers families post-birth home visits with baby items such as diapers and wipes. Hosts Every Child a Reader classes featuring group reading activities for children. Program is limited to 15 participants. 1445 SE 122nd Avenue Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: -Low income Black Diaspora, African Immigrant and Refugee pregnant individuals and new mothers aged 23 or younger -Diapers and wipes: Ages 0-18 months who are part of the Black Diaspora community Hours: Walk in: Tuesday/Friday 9am-5pm Phone: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Every Child a Reader Class: Thursday 10:30am-noon Email (503) 206-7672 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ALLCARE HEALTH ALLCARE HEALTH BROOKINGS OFFICE BABE STORE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF It is a rewards program where patients earn vouchers for healthy choices made, such as participating in classes and programs. Vouchers can be use at any of the Babe Stores to get items such as: baby clothing, blankets, sleepers, diapers, wipes, baby wash, infant bathtubs, washcloths, breast pads, nursing covers, nursing pillows, potty chairs, sippy cups, safetly latches and outlet protectors, and more. Eligibility: -Must be an AllCare CCO member to participate -Pregnant women and babies 0-3 years old Hours: Tuesday 12:30pm-4:30pm (541) 471-4106 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ALLCARE HEALTH ALLCARE HEALTH MAIN OFFICE BABE STORE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF It is a rewards program where patients earn vouchers for healthy choices made, such as participating in classes and programs. Details BIRTHRIGHT OF HILLSBORO PREGNANCY AND PARENTING SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A faith-based agency that provides information, support, and referrals to those experiencing unplanned pregnancies. Services include pregnancy testing, pro-life counseling, maternity and infant clothes, diapers, and other infant care needs. Does not recommend or refer to abortion services. 232 NE Lincoln Street Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Expectant families and new parents Hours: Tuesday 11am-6pm, Wednesday-Friday 11am-2pm, other times available by appointment Email (503) 648-6766 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BIRTHRIGHT OF SALEM PREGNANCY AND PARENT SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A faith-based organization that provides information and referrals to those experiencing unplanned pregnancies. Does not recommend or refer to abortion services. 1880 Lancaster Drive NE Suite 124 Salem, OR 97305 Eligibility: Expectant families and new parents Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 10am-1pm, City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Families Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-4pm (541) 524-2331 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES EUGENE CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES COMMUNITY SERVICE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with clothing, personal hygiene products, diapers, and showers. Helps connect individuals with other community resources and access mainstream benefits, including assistance with SNAP applications and local transit single-ride bus passes (which can be requested twice per month, subject to availability). Also, assists in completing forms to obtain ID and birth certificates, including limited payment assistance. Accepts donations of food, clothing, and other items. 1464 W 6th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 8am-4pm, Thursday 9am-4pm Email (541) 345-3628 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES SPRINGFIELD CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES COMMUNITY SERVICE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with clothing, personal hygiene products, diapers, and showers. Helps connect individuals with other community resources and access mainstream benefits, including assistance with SNAP applications and local transit single-ride bus passes (which can be requested twice per month, subject to availability). Also, assists in completing forms to obtain ID and birth certificates, including limited payment assistance. Accepts donations of food, clothing, and other items. 1025 G Street Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 8am-4pm, Thursday 9am-4pm Email (541) 345-3628 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS SERVICE CENTER PERSONAL GOODS AND DIAPERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, hygiene items as available, and household goods, including bedding and kitchenware. Diapers are available through PDX Diaper Bank. Accepts donations of gently used clothing and household items. 8800 SE 80th Avenue Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Clothing and Hygiene supplies: Tuesday-Thursday 10am-1pm Email (503) 771-7914 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLATSOP COMMUNITY ACTION PERSONAL CARE PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides hygiene and grooming supplies including; deodorant, soap, shaving cream, razors, toothbrushes, toothpaste, toilet paper, and diapers. May also have household cleaning products. Clients may inquire about other basic needs not covered by SNAP benefits. 364 9th Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Low-income households; can receive assistance once every 60 days Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-3:30pm Email (503) 325-1400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION RESOURCE ENTERPRISES INC EMERGENCY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with utility bills, one-time rent payments, and prescriptions. May have personal items such as clothes, hygiene products, diapers, and pet supplies, depending on donations received. 2310 1st Street Suite 2 Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm (503) 842-5261 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY SHARING PROGRAM BABY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides diapers and formula. Also offers access to used clothing, depending on availability. 1440 Birch Avenue Cottage Grove, OR 97424 Eligibility: Residents who self-identify as low income Hours: Monday 1:30pm-6pm, Tuesday-Friday 11am-3:30pm Email (541) 942-2176 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY NURTURING CENTER FAMILY NURTURING CENTER JACKSON COUNTY DIAPERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A small package of 12-15 diapers sizes 0-6 are available to clients and community members once per month per child in the family who is in diapers. Pull ups are limited. 825 E Main Street Suite A Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm, Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 779-5242 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY NURTURING CENTER FAMILY NURTURING CENTER JACKSON COUNTY LITTLE SPROUTS CLOTHING SWAP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides items such as childrens clothing sizes 0-8, diapers, wipes, toys, car seats, stroller, and food, depending on availabilty. 825 E Main Street Suite A Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-5pm, Friday 9am-noon Email (541) 779-5242 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY NURTURING CENTER FAMILY NURTURING CENTER JOSEPHINE COUNTY DIAPERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A small package of 12-15 diapers sizes 0-5 are available to clients and community members once per month per child in the family who is in diapers. Pull ups are limited. 214 W 4th Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday 10am-2pm, Wednesday/Friday 10am-noon 310 SW H Street, Grants Pass, OR 97526 Email (541) 779-5242 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY PROMISE OF LINCOLN COUNTY COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides diapers and personal care items (such as shampoo, toothbrushes, and deodorant) when available. Households may visit once per month. 5030 SE Highway 101 Lincoln City, OR 97367 Eligibility: Homeless and low-income families with children under age 18 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm, appointment required Email (541) 614-0964 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FISH OF ALBANY INC BASIC NEEDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides food boxes, clothing, shoes, bedding, personal and feminine hygiene products, diapers, baby food and formula, assistance in acquiring identification documents, gasoline and propane vouchers, and local bus tickets. Availability is subject to donations and eligibility. 1035 SE 2nd Avenue Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Food and clothing: By appointment only All other services: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 928-4460 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GG'S HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides blankets, a preselected bag of food, clothing, diapers, household items, cleaning supplies, and toiletries. Other items may be available when donations are received. Accepts donations of similar items and monetary donations. 1714 Pacific Avenue Forest Grove, OR 97116 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-1pm Email (503) 359-3871 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GOOD SHEPHERD HEALTH CARE SYSTEM CONNEXIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about community resources and case management which may include, basic needs, medical transportation, healthcare coverage, and medication management. Also provides diapers. 610 NW 11th Street Hermiston, OR 97838 Eligibility: Diapers: Families with children ages 0-3 All other services: vary by service Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 667-3504 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GROWING TOGETHER FAMILY COMMUNITY CENTER EMERGENCY DIAPER CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency diapers, baby formula, and baby wipes. Clients are eligible to visits once per week and each child is eligible for one pack of diapers or one pack of pull-ups. 310 SW H Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-5 Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday 10am-2pm, Wednesday/Friday 10am-noon Email (541) 864-0776 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOPE CHURCH BABY BOUTIQUE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides diapers, wipes, infant and toddler clothing (size 2T and under), and baby equipment. 2835 Santiam Highway SE Albany, OR 97322 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday 2:30pm-4:30pm, Wednesday 10am-noon Email (541) 967-4673 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOPE PREGNANCY CENTER PREGNANCY AND PARENT SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A faith-based agency that provides information and referrals to those who are experiencing unplanned pregnancies. Services include pro-life counseling, pregnancy testing, limited obstetrical ultrasound, parenting support, limited STI testing and community referrals. Does not provide referrals for abortion services or birth control. Clients who participate in the classes are eligible to receive maternity and infant clothes, diapers, and other infant care needs. 17 SW 6th Street Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Medical services: Monday/Thursday 10am-5pm, Wednesday 1pm-5pm Education day only, no medical services offered: Tuesday noon-5pm Email (541) 889-4272 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JOIN JOIN PDX Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a daytime drop-in center. Services include access to showers, bathrooms, mail pick up and PO Box setup, hygiene items, diapers, pet food, on-site WiFi and telephone usage, and specialized information and referral. 1435 NE 81st Avenue Suite 100 Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Unrestricted with priority given to homeless individuals or families Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Friday/Saturday/Sunday 9am-5pm, Tuesday/Thursday 10am-4pm. Closed on national holidays. Email (503) 232-2031 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LANE COUNTY DIAPER BANK DIAPERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides diapers and wipes. 3050 Game Farm Road Springfield, OR 97478 Eligibility: OHP, WIC, or SNAP recipients Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 221-0824 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC NEWBERG GAP MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects people with local churches and service agencies. Basic goods and services are offered to clients as they are donated. Services available may include, but are not limited to: bicycles, breakfast/lunch bags, clothing closets (adults and children), community meals, counseling services, diapers, haircuts, H.U.G.S. (hats, umbrellas, gloves, scarves, and socks), kitchen supplies, laundry service, bedding/linens, personal grooming supplies/toiletries, over-the-counter medications, personal care kits, physical therapy, showers, water bill assistance, and firewood. Accepts donations of bedding and linen, clothing, and personal grooming supplies. 200 S College Street Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-1pm Email (503) 537-3999 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC OF CLACKAMAS COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Community center for services and referrals to provide support and long-term resource connections. Provides diapers. Visitors are encouraged to participate in community classes, workshops, and relational-based support. Eligibility: Verification of need for some resources Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 10:30am-1pm Email (503) 650-0153 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC OF GREATER HILLSBORO GAP MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides essential household items, including beds and bedding, linens, furniture, clothing, diapers, hygiene products, cleaning and laundry supplies, paper goods, and work boots. Donations are accepted of the items the agency distributes, with an ongoing need for couches, kitchen tables, and queen-size or smaller beds (including mattresses, box springs, and frames). Referrals to local food pantries are available. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday noon-2:30pm, Tuesday/Thursday 9:30am-noon Email (503) 917-0205 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC OF LINN COUNTY GAP MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Basic goods are offered to clients as they are donated. Goods available may include, but are not limited to household items (cleaning supplies, laundry detergent, toilet paper, and paper towels), personal care supplies (such as toothbrushes and shampoo), durable medical goods, diapers and wipes, linens, towels and blankets, kitchenware (dishes, pots and pans, glasses, etc), and baby blankets. Connects people in need with local churches and service agencies. No financial assistance is available. 1606 Santiam Road SE Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Low income individuals and families Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Thursday 9am-1pm, Tuesday 5pm-8pm Email (541) 924-5683 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST A FAMILY PLACE MCMINNVILLE CLOTHING AND DIAPER BANK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides children's clothing, maternity clothing, and emergency diapers. Families can receive a 3-day supply of diapers once a month when supplies are available. 125 SE Cowls Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Families in need of diapers and clothing for children ages 0-5, or those who need maternity clothing Hours: Thursday 10am-noon Email (503) 472-4020 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST A FAMILY PLACE NEWBERG CLOTHING AND DIAPER BANK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides children's clothing, maternity clothing, and emergency diapers. Families can receive a 3-day supply of diapers once a month when supplies are available. 600 E 6th Street Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: Diapers and children's clothing: Families with children ages 0-5 Maternity clothing: Unrestricted Hours: Thursday 10am-noon Email (503) 472-4020 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST A FAMILY PLACE WILLAMINA CLOTHING AND DIAPER BANK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides children's clothing, maternity clothing, and emergency diapers. Families can receive a 3-day supply of diapers once a month when supplies are available. 1100 NE Oaken Hills Drive Willamina, OR 97396 Eligibility: Families with minor children ages 0-5 Hours: Thursday 10am-noon Email (503) 472-4020 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MANO A MANO FAMILY CENTER COLONIA AMISTAD PARENTING SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting support such as community cafes, the Casa Infantil Project, Strengthening Families Parenting Classes, advocacy, diapers, baby formula, and other baby items. 30 S Gun Club Road Independence, OR 97351 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday noon-6pm Email (503) 838-0689 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MANO A MANO FAMILY CENTER COLONIA LIBERTAD PARENTING SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting support such as community cafes, the Casa Infantil Project, Strengthening Families Parenting Classes, advocacy, diapers, baby formula, and other baby items. 2921 Saddle Club Street SE #1009 Salem, OR 97317 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday noon-6pm Email (503) 315-2290 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MANO A MANO FAMILY CENTER NORTHGATE CENTER PARENTING SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting support such as community cafes, the Casa Infantil Project, Strengthening Families Parenting Classes, advocacy, diapers, baby formula, and other baby items. 3850 Portland Road NE Suite 130 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday noon-6pm Email (503) 363-1895 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT GRANTS PASS RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to a food pantry, personal hygiene supplies, diapers, baby formula, sleeping bags, bedding, tents, pet food, laundry facilities, and computers for schoolwork or job searching. Also offers harm reduction workshops, advocacy, case management, temporary mailing addresses, and referrals to other community resources. 223 SE M Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or a parent or guardian of school-aged children or younger in their care. Must also be currently, recently, or about to be in unstable or unsafe housing, including couch surfing, staying in hotels, camping, living on the streets, living in a car or trailer, staying in a shelter or other temporary situation, or any other unsafe or dangerous living situation. If someone does not qualify, staff will work with them to find other community resources. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 841-1974 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT MEDFORD Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to a food pantry, personal hygiene supplies, diapers, baby formula, sleeping bags, bedding, tents, pet food, laundry facilities, and computers for schoolwork or job searching. Also offers harm reduction workshops, advocacy, case management, temporary mailing addresses, and referrals to other community resources. 500 Monroe Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or a parent or guardian of school-aged children or younger in their care. Must also be currently, recently, or about to be in unstable or unsafe housing, including couch surfing, staying in hotels, camping, living on the streets, living in a car or trailer, staying in a shelter or other temporary situation, or any other unsafe or dangerous living situation. If someone does not qualify, staff will work with them to find other community resources. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 608-6868 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MOTHER & CHILD EDUCATION CENTER CLOTHING AND BABY SUPPLIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides maternity clothing and supplies for pregnant women and nursing mothers. Also provides baby clothing, baby blankets, sleepers, toys and children's books, as well as emergency supplies of diapers, formula, and baby food. Strollers, infant bathtubs, car seats, and pack and plays available dependent on availability. Parents are encouraged to become a client (which is free) and allows access to more services. Diapers and formula are available for emergency needs only and not provided on an ongoing basis. Mother & Child is not a clothing closet; it is a program which has supplies to assist families but also focuses on education and support. 1515 NE 41st Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Pregnant or parents/primary caregivers of a child aged birth to 5 years old. No eligibility requirements based on income. The client must be able to come to the Mother and Child Education Center to receive services. Diapers, clothing, and other goods cannot be mailed or delivered to clients. Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-4pm, Friday 10am-2pm Email (503) 249-5801 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN REHABILITATION ASSOCIATION OF THE NORTHWEST NARA NW CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES GRESHAM CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health assessment and evaluation, counseling (individual, couples, family, and group), case management, cultural groups and activities, Native parenting classes, foster parent groups, access to physical health services, Healthy Native Kids (OHP/CHIPRA) insurance assistance, and wraparound care for Native youth with multiple needs. Referrals to other resources provided. Also provides diapers. Staff receive training on cultural competence to provide cultural programming for clients. Staff includes Master's-level counselors and social workers, certified alcohol and drug counselors (CADC), psychologists and child psychiatrists, and nurse practitioners. Student interns studying to become CADCs or counselors may also work under supervision of licensed staff. The program is certified by the State of Oregon to provide mental health and addiction treatment services for all ages. 620 NE 2nd Street Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Wraparound care: Youth who identify as Native American ages 9-21 All other services: Families with minor children who identify as Native American Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 953-6598 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEWMAN UNITED METHODIST CHURCH SINGLE PARENT MINISTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides limited, one time basic needs assistance. Assistance may include clothing, furniture, linens, towels and blankets, kitchenware (dishes, pots, pans, glasses, etc.), diapers and wipes, and personal hygiene supplies. One time limited assistance with utility payments such as telephone, water, electric, and gas. All payments are made directly to the vendor, and assistance is not guaranteed. The amount of assistance is on a case-by-case basis and current funding availability. 132 NE B Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Single parents with minor children Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-1pm Email (541) 479-5369 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTH COUNTY FOOD BANK NORTH TILLAMOOK COUNTY FOOD BANK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides food boxes with about a week’s worth of groceries, including fresh produce and basic kitchen staples such as cooking oil, sugar, and flour. Also offers cat and dog food, diapers for children and adults, and personal hygiene items. People can choose items from a list; selections vary based on availability. 230 Rowe Street Wheeler, OR 97147 Eligibility: Must meet USDA guidelines; no one is turned away Hours: Tuesday noon-3pm Email (503) 368-7724 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OLD MILL CENTER FOR CHILDREN AND FAMILIES RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Relief nursery providing services for at-risk families that include therapeutic classrooms for toddlers and preschoolers, home visiting, outreach and parent support groups. Resource closet available with food, children's clothing, diapers, wipes, hygiene items and cleaning supplies. 1650 SW 45th Place Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-6 who are low-income, a single-parent, teen parent, experiencing mental health or drug/alcohol issues, victims of trauma or domestic violence, involvement with child welfare, homeless or at risk of becoming homeless Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-5:30pm Email (541) 757-8068 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PDX DIAPER BANK CLOTH DIAPERING ON A BUDGET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a cloth diapering workshop which includes supplies to get participants started. Eligibility: Families receiving SNAP, WIC, or any other government subsidy are automatically eligible Hours: Varies by class Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details PDX DIAPER BANK DIAPER BANK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides diapers and incontinence supplies in collaboration with partner agencies. Eligibility: Varies by agency Hours: Varies by site Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details PHILOMATH COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shopping-style food pantry once per month. Gleaning program distributes gleaned surplus food weekly. Provides a children's clothing closet and diapers. Also offers a community garden and gardening instruction to community members.
